title: Week 16 - Project Life
excerpt: It was another busy week. Still loving every single bit of this and still trying to keep it fairly simple. And colorful, of course. The week started off with Buster doing a final photo shoot for the independent film he's…
body:
It was another busy week. Still loving every single bit of this and still trying to keep it fairly simple. And colorful, of course.

The week started off with Buster doing a final photo shoot for the independent film he's in. We got to see a rough cut of the short film and I have to say that I am super impressed with it. I will share more information when I can. JC got us each a Nike+ FuelBand and I am obsessed with it. We had delicious meals out and in, and I had a quick 1 night trip to Reno to teach at Paper Daisies in Sparks, NV. The owners are some of the sweetest in the business and they know how much I love to eat. We went to an institution called BJ's and had some delicious BBQ. Not to mention some Kick-Ass Fries. No, really, that is what they are called. And they ain't lyin', the fries were kick-ass.

I flew Southwest for the first time and quite enjoyed it. It was my only direct option home at the time I wanted on Saturday evening. The lining up thing wasn't all that bad - I even got a window seat in row 5 and was able to see Lake Tahoe since someone very sweet advised me to sit on the right side. As much as I loved my first Southwest experience, my loyalties still remain with Delta.

Supplies: white Journal Thickers, , airplane stamp, Sketchbook 6X6 paper pad, Sadie's Story paper, jillibean soup Alphabean letter stickers (my new fave), Studio Calico 6X6 paper pad,  Hambly herringbone overlay, mostly from Two Peas in A Bucket, some from the local independent stores I visited recently. You can see all my Project Life posts here. How are all of you doing with Project Life? My Q&A post on Project Life will have to come in the next couple weeks when I take some more process photos. Oh and there's been a big restock of the Project Life photo pocket pages on Amazon, in case you all missed out.
